Description: A single CMCC-style semantic mirror for the planar unit-distance theorem neighborhood. It treats concepts, structures, invariants, transformations, projections, formulas, and theorem anchors as first-class typed objects in a bitemporal ACID DAG intended for PostgreSQL testing.
design_principles: Use the final theorem as a fixed-point anchor, not as a proof target.
Model semantic topology densely enough that routes across domains are inspectable.
Separate semantic adjacency from derivation/evaluation dependencies; only dependency edges must be acyclic.
Represent infinite mathematical objects intensionally by definitions, invariants, and construction maps.
Store raw assertions; calculate derived consequences through explicit formulas and aggregations.
Make unknown, conjectural, or not-yet-modeled edges explicit rather than pretending they are absent.

contexts.Description: Mathematical or modeling context in which assertions are interpreted.

data.Description: The modeling context for this initial sketch.
Ordinary two-dimensional Euclidean geometry.

data.AssumptionsJSON: {"object":"finite point sets in R^2","edge_rule":"distance equals one"}
data.Description: Maximize the number of unit-distance pairs among n points in the plane.

data.AssumptionsJSON: {"target":"U(n) > n^(1+epsilon) infinitely often or for arbitrarily large n"}
data.Description: Context anchored by a superlinear construction for planar unit distances.

domains.Description: Top-level mathematical neighborhoods included in the single model.

data.Description: Types, relation signatures, field definitions, and temporal governance.
Points, configurations, distance, unit-distance predicates, transformations.
Unit-distance graphs, edges, edge counts, graph invariants, realizability.
Number fields, rings of integers, field norms, discriminants, prime ideals.
Minkowski embeddings, lattices, Gram matrices, projections into Euclidean spaces.
Asymptotic functions, exponents, lower bounds, construction families.
Final theorem anchors, versions, proof objects, and semantic route targets.

object_types.Description: Strong types for first-class mathematical objects. ParentTypeID gives a simple inheritance tree.

schema.name: ParentTypeID
schema.Description: FK to object_types.TypeID; nullable only for root.

data.Description: Root type for all objects.
Named conceptual object or semantic atom.
Object with internal parts, operations, relations, or axioms.
Collection or intensional family.
Typed map or formula object.
Boolean-valued typed function.
Quantity or property preserved by a class of transformations.
Map acting on objects while preserving or modifying structure.
Dimension-reducing or view-defining transformation.
Parametrized family of objects used to realize an asymptotic claim.
Mathematical claim with status and optional proof/provenance.
Inequality or asymptotic estimate.
The structure R^2 with its standard metric.
Point in an ambient space.
Finite set of points in the Euclidean plane.
Distance function on a space.
Predicate defined by distance constraints.
Vertices and edges, possibly induced by geometry.
Graph quantity independent of labeling.
Map from graph vertices into a geometric space.
Finite extension of Q.
Ring object such as the ring of integers of a number field.
Ideal inside a ring.
Prime ideal, often tracked by norm and splitting behavior.
Degree, discriminant, norm counts, ramification data.
Embedding of a number field into R or C.
Discrete subgroup of Euclidean space with basis/Gram data.
Matrix encoding inner products of a basis.
Canonical embedding of a number field/ring into Euclidean space.
Function considered up to growth behavior.
Asymptotic lower/upper bound or exponent statement.
Intermediate concept that connects two otherwise distant neighborhoods.

relation_types.Description: Typed edge kinds. IsDAGEdge means the edge participates in the acyclic dependency/evaluation graph.

data.Description: Type or specialization edge.
Constituent relation.
Reverse constituent relation; usually not used for dependency evaluation.
Definition dependency.
Reverse definition relation.
Dependency edge for evaluation and route explanations.
One structure canonically generates another.
Reverse induced relation.
Object is characterized by invariant.
Transformation preserves invariant or predicate.
Embedding into another structure/space.
Projection or coordinate map to another object.
Construction realizes a predicate, configuration, or theorem condition.
Quantity counts objects or relations.
Inequality or asymptotic bound targets a function/quantity.
Lower-bound relation to an asymptotic function.
Calculated field or derived object dependency.
Lookup-style dependency from one table/object to another.
Aggregation over a family or relation set.
Non-evaluative semantic adjacency across neighborhoods.
Analogy edge; not a dependency.
Anchor object pins a region of the semantic topology.

relation_type_signatures.Description: Allowed source and target type pairs. T_MATH_OBJECT acts as a broad supertype signature.

data.Description: Any object may be defined by another typed object.
Any object may depend on another object in the dependency DAG.
Finite point set induces its unit-distance graph.
Point set embeds in the Euclidean plane.
A graph invariant counts a graph feature.
Number fields have field invariants.
Ring of integers embeds into a lattice through a Minkowski embedding.
Lattice construction projects to a finite planar point set.
Construction family realizes an asymptotic bound.
Growth bound targets an asymptotic function.
A theorem anchors objects and routes in the topology.
Broad semantic bridge relation.

math_objects.Description: First-class mathematical objects. Infinite objects are encoded intensionally with descriptions/formal payloads.

data.Label: Euclidean plane R^2
data.Description: The ambient plane with standard Euclidean metric.

data.Label: Finite point configuration
data.Description: A finite set of n points in the Euclidean plane.

data.Label: Euclidean distance
data.Description: Standard Euclidean distance between two points.

data.Label: Unit-distance predicate
data.Description: Boolean predicate saying two points are exactly one unit apart.

data.Label: Unit-distance pair
data.Description: Unordered pair of points satisfying the unit-distance predicate.

data.Label: Unit-distance graph of a point set
data.Description: Graph whose vertices are points of P and whose edges are unit-distance pairs.

data.Label: Graph edge count
data.Description: Number of edges in a finite graph; equals unit-distance pair count for G(P).

data.Label: Maximum unit-distance count function
data.Description: Maximum number of unit-distance pairs determined by n planar points.

data.Label: Superlinear lower bound
data.Description: Existence of an exponent epsilon greater than zero giving superlinear lower growth along arbitrarily large n or a specified range.

data.Label: Explicit n^1.014 lower bound
data.Description: Explicit lower-bound anchor reported in a later writeup; useful as a stronger coordinate if adopted.

data.Label: Superlinear planar unit-distance theorem anchor
data.Description: The fixed-point theorem anchor for the semantic mirror.
data.FormalPayloadJSON: {"claim":"superlinear lower bound for planar unit distances","proof_status":"external/human-reviewed or to be attached"}

data.Label: Number field source object
data.Description: A finite extension of Q used as a source of structured algebraic data.

data.Label: Ring of integers
data.Description: Integral closure of Z in a number field K.

data.Label: Field degree
data.Description: Degree of the number field over Q.

data.Label: Field discriminant
data.Description: Discriminant controlling arithmetic/geometric complexity of K.

data.Label: Many small-norm prime ideals
data.Description: Arithmetic feature that can drive many short/algebraically controlled differences.

data.Label: Field norm
data.Description: Multiplicative norm from K to Q.

data.Label: Golod-Shafarevich criterion bridge
data.Description: Criterion appearing in arithmetic constructions with many small-norm primes and controlled discriminant.

data.Label: Minkowski embedding
data.Description: Embedding from algebraic integers into a Euclidean lattice representation.

data.Label: Minkowski lattice
data.Description: Lattice obtained by embedding the ring of integers into Euclidean space.

data.Label: Gram matrix of embedding
data.Description: Inner-product matrix controlling Euclidean distances in the lattice embedding.

data.Label: Short-vector/unit-edge relation
data.Description: Bridge object connecting algebraic difference structure to geometric unit edges.

data.Label: Planar projection/coordinate map
data.Description: Projection or coordinate selection mapping a higher-dimensional lattice construction into the plane.

data.Label: Algebraic unit-distance construction family
data.Description: Parametrized family of planar point configurations arising from algebraic/lattice data.

data.Label: Metric-to-edge bridge
data.Description: Semantic bridge from Euclidean metric predicates to graph adjacency.

data.Label: Field-to-lattice bridge
data.Description: Semantic bridge from algebraic integers to lattice points.

data.Label: Lattice-to-plane bridge
data.Description: Semantic bridge from high-dimensional lattice construction to planar configuration.

data.Label: Local-count-to-asymptotic bridge
data.Description: Semantic bridge from finite edge counts to asymptotic lower bounds.

math_relations.Description: Typed relation assertions. Valid/transaction ranges are represented as ISO strings in this sketch; PostgreSQL should use tstzrange.

data.Description: Finite planar configuration lives in the Euclidean plane.
Unit distance is defined using Euclidean distance.
A unit-distance pair is a pair satisfying the unit-distance predicate.
A point set induces its unit-distance graph.
Graph adjacency is defined by the unit-distance predicate.
Edge count counts unit-distance pairs.
U(n) aggregates maximum edge counts across all n-point configurations.
Superlinear bound targets U(n).
The theorem anchors the lower-bound claim.
Optional explicit n^1.014 coordinate for U(n).
Number field has degree.
Number field has discriminant.
Small-norm prime ideals are a key arithmetic multiplicity feature.
Ring of integers is defined relative to K.
O_K embeds in a Minkowski lattice.
The lattice is defined by the Minkowski embedding.
Gram matrix controls lattice distances.
Short vector/unit-edge relation is computed from Gram data and normalization.
Planar projection is defined on lattice construction data.
A finite portion/projection of lattice data yields a planar point set.
Construction family depends on a number field.
Construction family depends on projection to the plane.
Construction family realizes the superlinear lower bound.
Bridge points to the metric predicate side.
Bridge points to the graph adjacency side.
Bridge points to algebraic source.
Bridge points to lattice target.
Bridge points to lattice side.
Bridge points to planar point-set side.
Bridge points to finite edge counts.
Bridge points to asymptotic function.
Candidate bridge from existence criterion to small-norm prime abundance.

property_types.Description: Typed scalar/JSON attributes for objects and assertions.

data.Description: Dimension of ambient Euclidean or embedding space.
Number of inputs.
Number of points in a finite configuration.
Number of edges in a finite graph.
Exponent in a lower bound such as n^alpha.
Human/formal expression of the lower bound.
Numerical field degree when known.
Exact value or asymptotic class of discriminant.
Count of prime ideals below a norm threshold.
Matrix entries for basis inner products.
Shortest path distance in selected semantic/dependency graph.
known, candidate, conjectural, missing, or rejected.
External or internal source metadata.

property_values.Description: Initial attribute assertions. Use ValueNumber, ValueString, ValueBoolean, or ValueJSON depending on PropertyType.Datatype.

data.Description: Plane is two-dimensional.
Distance takes two point inputs.
Unit-distance predicate takes two point inputs.

data.ValueString: U(n) >= n^(1+epsilon) for some epsilon > 0, in the relevant asymptotic sense
data.Description: Generic superlinear lower bound statement.
Explicit exponent coordinate.

data.ValueString: U(n) > n^1.014 for arbitrarily large n / n sufficiently large depending on theorem formulation
data.Description: Expression should be reconciled with attached source/proof before promotion.
A likely intermediate semantic object, not yet formalized.
Known mathematical criterion, route details not encoded here.

formula_definitions.Description: Executable formula objects; formula language intentionally resembles the existing ERB pattern.

schema.name: FormulaKind
schema.Description: lookup, calculated, aggregation, predicate, or route_metric.

data.Description: Squared Euclidean distance for point-coordinate rows.
Practical exact/near-exact unit-distance predicate for finite computed data.
Counts unit-distance edges in a finite configuration.
Maximum edge count over n-point configurations.
Finite empirical exponent; useful for test data and candidate construction evaluation.
Positive margin indicates finite superlinear behavior.
Distance squared in a lattice basis using Gram matrix.
Semantic navigation distance to fixed-point theorem anchor.

semantic_routes.Description: Named route skeletons through the topology. These are not proofs; they are navigational spines.

data.Description: Point set → unit-distance predicate → unit-distance graph → edge count.
Number field → ring of integers → Minkowski lattice → projection → planar point configuration.
Finite edge counts across a construction family become an asymptotic lower-bound claim.
Algebraic source → lattice/geometry → graph counts → asymptotic lower bound → theorem anchor.

semantic_route_steps.Description: Ordered route steps; each step is a typed edge candidate that can be tested/refined.

data.Description: Point configuration induces graph.
Graph edge relation is defined by metric predicate.
Edge count summarizes induced graph.
Arithmetic feature supplies many controlled relationships.
Ring embeds as a lattice.
Gram matrix converts algebraic/lattice differences into Euclidean distances.
The edge-producing relation should be formalized here.
Projection/selection yields planar point configurations.
Across the construction family, edge count becomes a growth sequence.
Construction family realizes lower bound.
Bound applies to maximum unit-distance function.
The theorem object anchors the bound.

model_tests.Description: Tests an AI agent should implement first in PostgreSQL.

data.TestName: All relation assertions satisfy type signatures
data.QuerySketch: select relation_id from math_relations where not exists allowed signature through type ancestry
data.Description: Ensures strong typing of relation edges.

data.QuerySketch: recursive traversal over math_relations where IsDAGEdge=true; detect source reachable from target
data.Description: Semantic adjacency can cycle; dependency/evaluation edges cannot.

data.TestName: The theorem anchor is reachable from core domains
data.QuerySketch: shortest paths from OBJ_NUMBER_FIELD_K, OBJ_FINITE_POINT_SET, OBJ_UNIT_DISTANCE_GRAPH to OBJ_THEOREM_SUPERLINEAR_UNIT_DISTANCE
data.Description: Validates the single model is connected around the theorem anchor.

data.QuerySketch: build formula dependency graph from DependsOnObjectIDs and math_relations REL_COMPUTED_FROM/REL_LOOKUP_FROM/REL_AGGREGATES_OVER
data.Description: Ensures generated SQL views/functions can be ordered.

data.QuerySketch: select route steps where GapStatus in ('candidate','partial_candidate','candidate_direction_check','needs_direction_refinement')
data.Description: Ensures the model does not hide missing semantic infrastructure.
